speaker_segmentation.message: Method 3B improved ECAPA pipeline: central/adaptive VAD, overlapping chunks, ECAPA-TDNN speaker embeddings, optional automatic speaker-count estimation, clustering, isolated-label smoothing, short-segment merging, and clustering metrics.

interpretation.silhouette_score: Higher is better. It checks whether chunks inside the same cluster are similar and chunks from different clusters are separated.
interpretation.cluster_balance_ratio: Closer to 1.0 means more balanced speaker clusters. Very low values may indicate one dominant speaker cluster or a clustering problem.
interpretation.speaker_switches: Lower is usually smoother. Many switches may indicate unstable diarization.
interpretation.segment_smoothness: Closer to 1.0 means fewer rapid speaker-label changes across chunks.

speaker_segmentation.important_note: Speaker names are anonymous cluster labels. Compare predicted speakers with manual/RTTM references using label mapping, not by assuming speaker_1 is always the same real person.

standard_der_pyannote.message: Not requested. Set use_pyannote_metrics=true to try standard DER.

diarization_evaluation.important_note: DER combines missed speech, false alarm, and speaker confusion. Speaker labels are matched before scoring, so predicted speaker_1 does not need to equal reference speaker_1.

formants.note: Approximate LPC-based formant estimates. More reliable formants would require Praat/parselmouth.

jitter_shimmer.note: Approximate jitter/shimmer from frame-level f0 and RMS, not cycle-exact Praat measurements.

harmonic_to_noise_ratio.note: Approximate HNR proxy based on harmonic separation, not Praat HNR.

speaking_rate.note: Speaking rate proxy from syllable-like RMS peaks. True speaking rate requires transcript or syllable labels.

voice_onset_time.note: This is not true phonetic VOT. True VOT needs stop-consonant boundaries or forced alignment.
